message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The reporter describes a security bug in `Azure.AI.Projects` where OAuth Identity Passthrough token/consent context is not isolated per signed-in user — after User A completes consent, subsequent users appear to run under that same token until expiry. A related open issue (#57923) was filed by the same author asking about session scoping for the same OAuth passthrough scenario.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects` (with `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI` in the response stream path), no explicit version listed
**Affected API:** AI Agents + MCP tool invocation with OAuth Identity Passthrough
**Scenarios:**
- User A signs in, triggers a tool call requiring OAuth consent, completes consent — tool calls succeed
- User B signs in and triggers the same tool call path — but appears to run with User A's already-consented token context instead of going through independent consent/token acquisition
- Attempting to force per-user delegated token acquisition throws `MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException` wrapping `MsalUiRequiredException` (user_null: "No account or login hint was passed to the AcquireTokenSilent call")

**Root ask:** Token/consent state must be strictly isolated per authenticated user so that each user acquires and runs under their own delegated identity, with no cross-user token reuse.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

- Mitigation attempts (per-user agents, MCP server restart) did not change the behavior, pointing to an issue upstream of the MCP server — likely in how the SDK or the Foundry service stores/retrieves the OAuth consent/token state
- The `MsalUiRequiredException` (user_null) on silent token acquisition suggests no per-user hint is being passed when trying to acquire a Foundry-scope delegated token
- The issue could be in how the SDK associates consent state with a session/agent instance rather than a per-request authenticated user context
- Related open issue: #57923 (same author, same OAuth passthrough scenario, filed one day earlier as a question)

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Verify how the SDK or Foundry service keys consent/token state — inspect whether it uses a per-user identifier (OID/UPN) or a shared key (agent ID, server session)
2. Confirm whether passing an explicit `userId` or user OBO token into the agent run/thread API would isolate the token context
3. Investigate the `AcquireTokenSilent` call path for Foundry scope — confirm the correct per-user `IAccount` hint is being passed and not a cached global/shared account
4. Check whether the SDK exposes any hook to inject a per-request credential/token provider for MCP tool calls requiring Identity Passthrough
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — the issue is with `Azure.AI.Projects`, a client SDK; behavior is described in terms of SDK-level token handling, not the underlying REST API
- **Service:** `AI Projects` — explicitly named as `Azure.AI.Projects` in the library name field; confirmed by the `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/` directory and matching label color (#e99695)
- **Confidence:** High — both category and service are unambiguously stated in the issue body; confirmed by quality reference patterns in similar issues (e.g., #57923)
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Projects` (line 100) — matched because the issue's service label is "AI Projects"
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @dargilco @nick863 @trangevi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" and "needs-team-attention" labels; @mentioned all ServiceOwners via routing comment
- **Scan notes:** Scanned from end of CODEOWNERS upward; entries for `%Mgmt`, `%OpenAI`, `%AI Model Inference`, `%AI Agents`, `%BatchAI` were encountered but none matched "AI Projects" as the sole required label; the `%AI Projects` entry at line 100 was the first full match
</details>
